epic.name: Market Focus
epic.story: As a user, I want to see how much target agencies spend in my capability areas so I can identify attractive markets.
As a user, I want to identify the largest prime contractors in my target markets so I can evaluate teaming targets.
As a user, I want to see which contracts may recompete in 6, 12, and 24 months so I can plan outreach early.
As a user, I want active SAM opportunities scored by relevance so I can prioritize immediate actions.
As a user, I want a capture notes log so I can track BD context, contacts, and next actions.

functional_requirements.requirement: Provide dashboard navigation in Streamlit sidebar.
Filter all pages by agency, office, vendor, NAICS, PSC, capability keyword, date range, and score band where applicable.
Calculate opportunity, teaming, and recompete scores using configurable weights.
Allow export of filtered tables to CSV.
Provide a manual capture log for notes, next steps, contact names, and outreach status.
Separate ingestion scripts from dashboard runtime.

nonfunctional_requirements.requirement: Dashboard must not perform live API calls during normal usage.
API keys must use environment variables loaded from .env and excluded by .gitignore.
Use DuckDB for fast local analytical queries.
Use Plotly charts and pandas dataframes in Streamlit.
Support Windows-first local development with cross-platform compatibility.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Running streamlit run app.py opens the dashboard.
Running python scripts/build_db.py creates data/govcon_radar.duckdb from sample CSVs.
Dashboard works after deleting sample data and replacing it with schema-matching API outputs.
